pool_stats.note: No LIMK or kinase-annotated compounds in DB. All 21K compounds mapped to SMA modifier targets (CAST, ANK3, PLS3, etc). Top candidates selected by potency + drug-likeness for kinase-class repurposing.

candidates.rationale: Highest potency in DB (pChEMBL=11). Diaminopteridine scaffold. Low logP good for CNS.
Pyrazolopyridine is a privileged kinase scaffold. Strong IC50. Near MW upper limit but viable.
Compact diaminopyrimidine — classic kinase inhibitor pharmacophore. Excellent MW and logP for CNS. High potency (Ki ~100pM).
Isomer of CHEMBL369258. Same diaminopyrimidine kinase scaffold. Excellent drug-likeness for CNS.
4-Anilinoquinazoline = EGFR/kinase inhibitor scaffold (erlotinib class). Strong candidate for LIMK2 binding.
Acrylamide warhead = covalent kinase inhibitor (afatinib-class). Could form irreversible bond with LIMK2 Cys residue.
Another diaminopyrimidine variant. Good potency and CNS-compatible properties.
Tert-butyl substituted diaminopyrimidine. Bulky group may improve selectivity for LIMK2 over LIMK1.
Hydroxyethylamine quinazoline. Lower logP = better CNS aqueous solubility.
Purine scaffold with histamine-like sidechain — classic kinase ATP-competitive motif. Strong LIMK2 docking candidate.
Tetrahydropyrimidine-glutamate. Very hydrophilic (logP=1.3). May bind LIMK2 allosteric site.
Xanthine derivative with cyclohexylamine. PDE inhibitor scaffold — kinase cross-reactivity possible.
Fourth diaminopyrimidine variant — scaffold enrichment signals real kinase activity.
Hydroxyphenyl variant of rank 12. Free phenol may form H-bond with LIMK2 hinge region.
Maleamic acid on quinazoline. Carboxylate may interact with LIMK2 catalytic lysine.
Neopentyl diaminopyrimidine. Fifth variant of this high-potency scaffold class.
Vinyl sulfone covalent warhead on quinazoline. Different covalent mechanism than rank 6.
Cyanoquinoline with benzylamine. Nitrile as hinge H-bond acceptor. Good MW for fragment growth.
Imidazole-quinazoline with terminal alkyne. Click chemistry handle for future SAR.
Minimal MW aminopyrimidine-quinazoline hybrid. Excellent CNS properties (MW=331, logP=2.97). Strong kinase hinge binder.

diaminopyrimidines.note: Dominant scaffold class (ranks 3,4,7,8,13,16). Classic kinase inhibitor pharmacophore. MW ~283 Da, excellent CNS properties. HIGH PRIORITY for LIMK2.

anilinoquinazolines.note: EGFR-class kinase inhibitor scaffold. Multiple warhead variants (reversible, acrylamide, vinyl sulfone). Bromophenyl aniline is key feature.

cyanoquinolines.note: Nitrile-bearing quinoline scaffolds. Good for hinge-binding to kinases.

xanthine_pde.note: PDE inhibitor scaffold. May have kinase cross-reactivity.

known_limk_inhibitors_for_comparison.name: BMS-5
known_limk_inhibitors_for_comparison.note: Known LIMK1/2 dual inhibitor. Already in docking campaign. Include as positive control.

recommendation: Top strategy: (1) Dock all 20 candidates + BMS-5 control against LIMK2 crystal structure (PDB: 4TPT). (2) Prioritize diaminopyrimidines (6 compounds, MW~283) as lead series. (3) Include covalent quinazolines (ranks 6,17) to test irreversible binding. (4) Compare docking scores to BMS-5 as benchmark.
